Callbacks: 
“This is (your name) from Georgia Urology returning your call. How can | help you?” 
Callback Voicemails: 
“This is Georgia Urology returning your call if you still need assistance please call us back, we 
are experiencing longer than normal wait times but we ask that you hold for an agent. We 
appreciate your patience and thank you for choosing Georgia Urology. 
Outbound: 
“Good morning/Good afternoon this is (your name) with Georgia Urology. Am | speaking with 
Mr./Ms. ?” 
Standard Greeting (Inbound Calls): 
Thank you for calling Georgia Urology Patient Support Center. This is (your name). How can | 
assist? 
Wrap Up: 
“Is there anything else | can help you with? Thank you again for calling Georgia Urology.”
Dropped Calls (due to know technology issues): 
“Thank you for calling Georgia Urology. This is (your name). | am sorry we just experienced a 
system issue that resulted in us losing calls. | apologize for any inconvenience this may have 
cause you. How can | help you?” 
Dead Air: > Use the standard phone greeting- Wait 10 seconds 
» Do asecond abbreviated greeting- “Georgia Urology this is (your name)”- Wait 
another 10 seconds 
> Simply say “Hello”- Wait another 10 seconds

» Do asecond abbreviated greeting- “Georgia Urology this is (your name)”- Wait 
another 10 seconds 
> Simply say “Hello”- Wait another 10 seconds 
» End the call with the following statement: “Disconnecting call due to no 
response. “ 
Deceased/Expired Patients: 
“I'am very sorry for your recent loss and am glad to assist during this difficult period. | can 
cancel the patient’s/your (specific relation)’s upcoming appointment at this time. May | ask the 
date of passing for my records? (Wait for response) Thank you. (Cancel appointments) All 
upcoming appointments have been cancelled. Is there any other way | can assist you? (Wait for 
response) Thank you for calling Georgia Urology, we express our condolences to you during this 
time.” 
Dismissed Patient: 
“I'am unable to schedule your appointment at this time due to a lock on your account, but if | 
could get your best contact information, I'll be happy to reach out to the proper parties to see 
what we can do about unlocking your account.” 
DASH New System Response: 
“l apologize this is taking a bit longer than usual, we implemented a new scheduling system 
recently and | am still getting comfortable with the new process. You can expect the next time 
you schedule an appointment; it will be much smoother and easier. ” 
DASH Connect Closing: 
“In the next 30 minutes you'll receive an email confirmation with your appointment details. Three to 
two days prior, you'll receive an email and text with your online check-in through our system called, 
Phreesia. Please complete this upon arrival. 
Available Dates in DASH Self: 
"We understand that you were on our website and saw the available as soon as date, however, this is 
the earliest availability throughout our practice and may not be available with the provider or 
appointment type you are requesting. | apologize for any confusion this may have caused; I'll be happy 
to continue scheduling your appointment." 
DASH Guarantor (PEDS only): 
“May | have the full name and dob of the person that will be legally responsible and will serve 
as the guarantor?”

Circumcision Consult: 
When scheduling a circumcision consultation, the following information needs to be reviewed 
with the parent, caregiver, or physician’s office after you have confirmed the patient’s age. 
“I would like to clarify that this appointment is a consultation with the physician, surgery will 
not be performed on this day. Our physicians perform Circumcisions once the child is 6 months
or older based on availability unless urgent or medically necessary. We perform circumcisions in 
an outpatient or hospital setting under general anesthesia. The physician will perform a 
physical exam & discuss the scheduling process during your consultation.” 
**Disclaimer: If the child is under 3 months and the parents request to be seen sooner read the 
script below: 
We are happy to schedule your appointment sooner, however a follow-up appointment may be 
necessary prior to scheduling the procedure. **schedule the appointment 2 to 3 weeks out*** 
Foster Parents/Pending Adoptions: 
“We do not perform circumcision for religious reasons. We perform circumcisions in an 
outpatient or hospital setting under general anesthesia. This requires an initial consultation to 
determine if the patient is a good surgical cancidate. Scheduling the process can take anywhere 
from 6-8 weeks after the consultation and the patient must be 6 months of age or older.” 
We want to be informed but these scenarios should be few and far between. 
Orthodox Jewish practice: 
A mohel (moil/a person who performs the Jewish rite of circumcision) handles newborn 
circumcisions- especially in the religious community 
If there is a physical or medical problem, then the mohel will tell family to see a pediatric 
urologist. This does not mean that we will do circ on day 8 of life. We just weigh in as to 
whether the mohel can do it or not. 
Dr. Scherz provided names of two mohel’s- We can give the names, but the caller will have to

whether the mohel can do it or not. 
Dr. Scherz provided names of two mohel’s- We can give the names, but the caller will have to 
research and reach out to them on their own. We aren’t making referrals just helpful 
information to further assist the caller. 
» Dr. Arthur Gumer 
» Dr. Jonathan Metzl 
If there is a medical problem that prevents the mohel from doing the circumcision our physician 
will perform the procedure using our current guidelines. It will not be expedited but the Rabbi 
**may** be present during the procedure to say the required prayers. The parents would have 
to discuss this with the physician during the consultation. The Patient Support Center cannot 
guarantee this will occur they can just advise the parents to discuss with the provider.
¢ If the parent declines, use the listed rebuttals: 
(1) “I understand your concerns, but our physicians would like your child to be seen in the 
HAWK clinic first, and only if there is a surgical reason would your child need to be seen 
by one of our Pediatric Urology Surgeons.” 
(2) “Let me walk you through what our HAWK clinic has to offer via our website 
gaurology.com. Our Nurse Practitioners have a wealth of knowledge dealing with all 
types of voiding dysfunctions and they publish blogs on the subject on a regular basis.” 
Vasectomy Consult: 
“I would like to clarify that this appointment is a consultation with the physician. This visit is for you to 
discuss the details of the vasectomy procedure and receive clarification of any questions you may have. 
If you decide to move forward with the procedure after your consultation, the physician will then 
discuss the scheduling process. The procedure can usually be scheduled for 6-8 weeks later.” 
Waitlist: 
“You'll receive an email confirming that you have been added to the waitlist. If an earlier appointment 
becomes available our system will send you a text message to move up your appointment, but you only 
have 15 minutes to respond. If you fail to respond within that timeframe the system will add you to the 
end of the list.”
GEONGIA LNOLOGY

end of the list.”

Circumcision Consult Script 
When scheduling a circumcision consultation, determine the patient's age prior to getting 
demographics or reviewing the script. 
“I would like to clarify that this appointment is a consultation with the physician, surgery will not be 
performed on this day. Our physicians perform Circumcisions once the child is 6 months or older 
based on availability unless urgent or medically necessary. We perform circumcisions in an 
outpatient or hospital setting under general anesthesia. The physician will perform a physical exam 
& discuss the scheduling process during your consultation.” 
**Disclaimer: If the child is under 3 months and the parents request to be seen sooner read the 
script below: 
We are happy to schedule your appointment sooner, but a follow-up appointment may be 
necessary before scheduling the procedure. **schedule the appointment 2 to 3 weeks out*** 
Foster Parents/Pending Adoptions: We will see the patient for the circumcision consult, however, 
unless urgent or medically necessary, we cannot proceed with the circumcision until the adoption 
has been finalized and/or they've returned to their parent’s custody.
The Newborn Circumcision Center 
e Who — Healthy, newborn baby boys up to 6 weeks of age & less than 13lbs (there is 
a screening process in place to determine eligibility) 
e What - Thisis a newborn circumcision center offering self-pay services only. TNCC 
does not accept insurance. The cost is $950 & it is due in full 72hrs before the 
procedure. 
e When — ‘Soft’ launch is Tuesday 10/18/22 
e Where — 11835 Alpharetta Hwy, Roswell GA 30076 
e Why — We have identified a need in our community to provide this as birth 
hospitals increasingly deny newborn circumcisions for questionable indications & 
fewer insurance companies cover ‘elective’ circumcisions. 
e Scheduling — The PSC does not schedule this clinic, we provide parents with the 
direct number

fewer insurance companies cover ‘elective’ circumcisions. 
e Scheduling — The PSC does not schedule this clinic, we provide parents with the 
direct number 
e Phone number: 678.208.6288 or extension 7090 
Circumcisions for Religious Reasons 
This is information that we want to share BUT we should only discuss with callers if they bring up the subject. 
“We do not perform circumcisions for religious reasons. We perform circumcisions in an outpatient or hospital setting under general 
anesthesia. This requires an initial consultation to determine if the patient is a good surgical candidate. Scheduling the procedure 
can take anywhere from 6-8 weeks after the consultation and the patient must be 6 months of age or older.” 
We want to be informed but these scenarios should be few and far between. 
Orthodox Jewish practice: 
A mobhel (/moil/a person who performs the Jewish rite of circumcision) handles newborn circumcisions - especially in the religious 
community 
If there is a physical or medical problem, then the mohel will tell family to see a pediatric urologist 
This does not mean that we will do circ on day 8 of life. We just weigh in as to whether the mohel can do it or not 
Dr. Scherz provided names of two mohel’s - We can give the names but the caller will have to research and reach out to them on 
their own. We aren’t making referrals just helpful information to further assist the caller. 
e Dr. Arthur Gumer 
* Dr.Jonathan Metz| 
If there is a medical problem that prevents the mohel from doing the circumcision our physicians will perform the procedure using 
our current guidelines. It will not be expedited but the Rabbi **may** be present during the procedure to say the required prayers. 
The parents would have to discuss this with the physician during the consultation. The Patient Support Center cannot guarantee this 
will occur they can just advise the parents to discuss it with the provider. ~ **This will ultimately be at the discretion of the physician 
and the surgical facility. IE: Covid is beyond our control... | doubt ORs are allowing Rabbi’s to be present given the current pandemic 
& risks. **

and the surgical facility. IE: Covid is beyond our control... | doubt ORs are allowing Rabbi’s to be present given the current pandemic 
& risks. **
"This is Georgia Urology returning 
your call if you still need 
assistance please call us back, we 
are experiencing longer than 
normal wait times but we ask that 
you hold for an agent. We 
appreciate your patience and 
thank you for choosing Georgia 
Urology.”
1. Confirm Legal Guardian: 
For the first visit, we require that the legal guardian schedule and accompany the child to 
the appointment; Are you the legal guardian or do you have legal rights to make medical 
decisions for the patient? 
If YES: 
Great! Let’s go ahead and get the appointment scheduled. 
If they confirm they are the legal guardian but cannot attend the initial visit: 
We prefer that the legal guardian accompanies the child to their first 
appointment. However, if you’re unable to attend we can accept a signed and dated letter 
of consent giving another individual permission to bring your child in for the visit. 
The letter must include: 
* Your full legal name (as the guardian) 
« The full legal name of the person you’re authorizing 
* Aclear statement granting permission for them to bring the child for the initial 
appointment 
* Your signature and the date along with a copy of your driver’s license 
The person you authorize to accompany your child must be 18 years or older and have 
avalid government-issued photo ID (such as a driver’s license, state ID, or passport) 
that exactly matches the name in your letter.) 
If NOT, the legal guardian: 
I’d be happy to schedule the appointment directly with them. Would you like to provide 
their contact information so we can reach out, or would you prefer to have them call us at 
404.252.25067" 
Pushback from caller: 
Determine the following - Will the legal guardian/parent bring the child to the 
appointment? 
« If YES: Proceed with scheduling, confirming that all demographic 
information is for the legal guardian/parent.

Determine the following - Will the legal guardian/parent bring the child to the 
appointment? 
« If YES: Proceed with scheduling, confirming that all demographic 
information is for the legal guardian/parent. 
« If NO: Contact the guardian/parent to provide letter details (see above). If 
they cannot be contacted, do not proceed. Offer to call back or provide a 
number for them to call. 
Note: Incarcerated Parents - They must sign documents allowing temporary medical 
decision rights. If your caller indicates they don’t have this information you cannot more 
forward to schedule an appointment.
*+**+Special Circumstances - urgent/ emergent***** 
In cases of urgent medical needs (such as testicular torsion or obstructing kidney stones 
etc.) someone other than the legal guardian may call to schedule the appointment. 
In these cases: 
* Create the patient's chart. 
* Notify Floor Support and explain the full situation 
« Escalate to Ped’s Triage- stay on the line with the caller and clearly explain to Triage the 
situation/ urgency. 
This ensures that urgent issues are never delayed while still following chain-of-command 
and protecting the child’s care. 
** If you’re unsure whether it’s a true urgent case, always check with Floor Support 
before proceeding.

NGPG Updates: 
The physicians and team at NGPG Urology are joining Georgia Urology Jan 1st, 2025: 
If a NGPG patient is calling about a 2024 appointment. Please direct them back to NGPG - 
That number is 770.219.8765 (for all NGPG locations). They will need to hit option # 1 and 
then option #1 once they get into the appointment scheduling prompt. 
We want to convey to patients that while the practice name changes, they will continue to 
receive the same excellent care from the physicians and our Care team! 
Who are the providers joining? 
« Ajay Gopalakrishna, MD

receive the same excellent care from the physicians and our Care team! 
Who are the providers joining? 
« Ajay Gopalakrishna, MD 
« Devendar Katkoori, MD 
« Kalpit Patel, MD 
« David Stanley, MD 
« Annie Nguyen, AGCACNP 
« Loading providers in NextGen: 
Please make sure to load the new providers in the NextGen environment. 
Label new Tab as**Gwinnett Northeast** to help familiarize yourself with the new 
providers. This will make it easier in the upcoming months. 
e We will begin importing existing patient appointments from the NGPG system into 
NextGen this weekend. The goal is to ensure that all patient appointments remain intact 
and consistent as we transition them into our system and make the process seamless. 
« Although we will begin receiving calls next week, we can only schedule 2025 
appointments 
Key Points to Remember: Dr. Pareek and David Johnson will not, but we will be taking over 
their patient lists.

Even though these patients are new to Georgia Urology, they are not new to their providers. 
We need to make sure the transition feels smooth and welcoming.
When answering calls from NGPG patients, here’s an example of a warm greeting you can use: 
“Thank you for calling Georgia Urology, this is [Agent’s Name] with the Patient Support 
Center. How can | help you today?” 
* Once the patient is identified as a former NGPG patient, continue with something like: 
“Mr./Mrs. [Patient’s Last Name], we’d like to welcome you to Georgia Urology and thank you 
for trusting us with your care. Let’s go ahead and confirm your upcoming appointment or get 
your appointment scheduled. (more verbiage examples to come)
